title: Replace chat regex filter with structured message classification
description:
## Problem
The Mineflayer adapter classifies server/system chat by matching a pattern list (`SYSTEM_MESSAGE_PATTERNS`) and checking for empty usernames. This regex firewall is brittle: any server-side phrasing change can leak noise into the LLM pipeline or suppress legitimate player messages.

## Context
From the deep code audit (Finding 3, 86% confidence): "The chat/system-message filter is still a regex firewall." The repo's own sprint notes call structured message classification a deferred item. This is an open architectural gap.

## Requirements
1. Classify chat messages by event source and semantic type where possible (Mineflayer's `bot.chat` events distinguish player messages, system messages, whispers, etc.)
2. Keep regexes only as a fallback boundary, not the primary classifier
3. Emit an explicit `messageKind` field (`player`, `system`, `whisper`, `teleport`, `join`, `leave`, etc.) so the C# side can reason about chat provenance without re-parsing text
4. Verify no false positives/negatives with current server output patterns

## References
- `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` lines 288-303 (SYSTEM_MESSAGE_PATTERNS)
- Audit Finding 3: "Chat/system-message filter is still a regex firewall"
- `Data/Pages/Audit/msa_planning_mineflayer_audit_20260621_1437.md`
